Logical Sieve - Benchmarks 

This tool expands on the work of Zwetsloot et al. (2021) to incorporate
the following changes:

1.  Ranking of biological, chemical, physical, and environmental parameters and processes related to soil health.
2.  Different land uses (agriculture, forest, and urban).
3. Ten climate zones (Mediterranean south (MDS), Mediterranean north (MDN), Mediterranean mountains (MDM), Lusitanian (LUS), Pannonian (PAN), Atlantic central (ATC), Continental (CON), Atlantic north (ATN), Nemoral (NEM), Boreal (BOR)). 
4. Two-tier pertinence evaluation: Tier-1 to select context-dependent soil health parameters and processes; Tier-2 to select context-dependent soil health indicators and methods based on extended technical criteria.

Developers

-   L. Chavez Rodriguez, Soil biology Group, Wageningen University,
    Wageningen, The Netherlands
-   C. Vazquez Martin, Soil biology Group, Wageningen University,
    Wageningen, The Netherland

Contributors

-   M.J. Zwetsloot, Soil biology Group, Wageningen University,
    Wageningen, The Netherlands
-   G. Bongiorno, Soil biology Group, Wageningen University, Wageningen,
    The Netherlands
-   R.E. Creamer, Soil biology Group, Wageningen University, Wageningen,
    The Netherlands
-   D.P. Di Lonardo, Soil biology Group, Wageningen University,
    Wageningen, The Netherlands

Related publication:

Zwetsloot, M. J., Bongiorno, G., Barel, J. M., Di Lonardo, D. P., & Creamer, R. E. (2022). A flexible selection tool for the inclusion of soil biology methods in the assessment of soil multifunctionality. Soil Biology and Biochemistry, 166, 108514. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.soilbio.2021.108514

*This dataset is published under the CC BY (Attribution) license. This
license allows reusers to distribute, remix, adapt, and build upon the
material in any medium or format, so long as attribution is given to the
creator*.
